_doc: Labeled merge / keep-separate pairs for tuning Phase D's cosine threshold τ.
_usage: Run `decisionlab.canonicalize._verify_merge` (or directly compute Voyage cosine) over each pair and compare against `should_merge`. Precision and recall on this set inform whether τ=0.85 holds or needs ratcheting (per plan §3, target ≥ 0.9 each at τ=0.85).

pairs.label: Paradigm

candidate.name: Q-learning
candidate.description: Off-policy temporal-difference reinforcement learning with action-value estimates

existing.name: Reinforcement Learning
existing.description: Reward-driven learning paradigm where an agent updates a policy from interaction-derived rewards

pairs.rationale: Q-learning is a child of reinforcement-learning; per Canonicalizer prompt, both should canonicalize to the umbrella RL slug.

existing.name: Reinforcement Learning
existing.description: Reward-driven learning paradigm where an agent updates a policy from interaction-derived rewards

pairs.label: Paradigm

existing.name: Prospect Theory
existing.description: Decision-making under risk where losses are weighted more heavily than equivalent gains

pairs.rationale: Different traditions, different mechanisms (reward maximization vs. risk-weighted utility).

pairs.label: Paradigm

candidate.name: Loss aversion
candidate.description: Asymmetric weighting of losses vs. gains in choice

existing.name: Prospect Theory
existing.description: Decision-making under risk where losses are weighted more heavily than equivalent gains

pairs.rationale: Loss aversion is a defining feature of prospect theory's value function — same paradigm under a different name.

pairs.label: Paradigm

candidate.name: Drift-diffusion model
candidate.description: Evidence-accumulation model for two-alternative forced-choice decisions

existing.name: Race model
existing.description: Independent accumulator decision model where multiple evidence streams race to a threshold

pairs.rationale: Architecturally distinct: shared latent variable in DDM vs. independent accumulators in race models.

pairs.label: Paradigm

candidate.name: Sequential sampling model
candidate.description: Decision-making by accumulating noisy evidence over time

existing.name: Drift-diffusion model
existing.description: Evidence-accumulation model for two-alternative forced-choice decisions

pairs.rationale: DDM is the prototypical sequential sampling model; same paradigm under a more general name.

pairs.label: Paradigm

candidate.name: Free-energy principle
candidate.description: Active inference and minimization of variational free energy

existing.name: Reinforcement Learning
existing.description: Reward-driven learning paradigm where an agent updates a policy from interaction-derived rewards

pairs.rationale: Different objective functions — RL maximizes reward, FEP minimizes prediction error.

pairs.label: Paradigm

candidate.name: Bounded rationality
candidate.description: Decision-making under cognitive and time constraints, satisficing rather than optimizing

existing.name: Heuristics-and-biases program
existing.description: Decision-making via cognitive shortcuts that systematically bias judgments

pairs.rationale: Heuristics are the operationalization of bounded rationality — same paradigm at different abstraction levels.

pairs.label: Variable

candidate.name: expected_reward
candidate.description: Average reward expected from taking an action

pairs.rationale: Same concept; underscore vs. space normalisation.

pairs.label: Variable

candidate.name: reward signal
candidate.description: Scalar reward emitted by the environment after an action

pairs.rationale: "Reward" and "reward signal" denote the same RL variable; the slight rephrasing is what fragments the registry pre-rewrite.

pairs.label: Variable

candidate.name: discount_factor
candidate.description: Coefficient γ that downweights future rewards

existing.name: learning_rate
existing.description: Coefficient α that controls update magnitude

pairs.rationale: γ and α are distinct hyperparameters with different roles in the Bellman update.

pairs.label: Variable

candidate.name: drift_rate
candidate.description: Rate at which evidence accumulates toward a decision threshold

existing.name: diffusion_coefficient
existing.description: Variance of the noise term in evidence accumulation

pairs.rationale: Drift and diffusion are paired but distinct parameters of the DDM.

pairs.label: Variable

candidate.name: value_function
candidate.description: v(s): expected return from state s under the current policy

existing.name: Q_function
existing.description: Q(s,a): expected return from state s after taking action a

pairs.rationale: V and Q functions are related but distinct — V depends only on state, Q on state-action pair.

candidate.name: internal_setpoint
candidate.description: Target reference value for a homeostatic variable

existing.name: set_point
existing.description: Target reference value an organism regulates toward

pairs.label: Postulate

candidate.statement: Agents act to maximize expected discounted reward.

existing.statement: Decision-makers select actions that maximize expected utility under risk.

pairs.rationale: Reward and utility are distinct constructs; reward is a one-shot environmental signal, utility includes risk preferences.

candidate.statement: The agent's policy is updated by gradient ascent on expected return.

existing.statement: Policy improvement proceeds via gradient ascent on the expected discounted return.

pairs.label: Postulate

candidate.statement: Choice probability is a softmax over action values.

existing.statement: Action selection follows the Boltzmann distribution over Q-values.

pairs.rationale: Softmax and Boltzmann are mathematically identical.

pairs.label: Postulate

candidate.statement: Variations in homeostatic state drive corrective actions.

existing.statement: Reward-prediction errors update value estimates via TD learning.

pairs.rationale: Different paradigms (homeostatic regulation vs. RL) addressing different drivers of behavior.
